concepts.name: Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure where elements are not stored at contiguous memory locations.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: The basic building block of a linked list, containing data and a pointer to the next node.

concepts.name: Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A variable that stores the memory address of another variable.

concepts.name: Head
concepts.short_definition: The first node in a linked list.

concepts.name: Tail
concepts.short_definition: The last node in a linked list.

concepts.name: Next Pointer
concepts.short_definition: The pointer within a node that refers to the subsequent node in the list.

concepts.name: Null Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer that does not point to any valid memory location, often indicating the end of a list.

concepts.name: Cycle
concepts.short_definition: A condition in a linked list where a node's next pointer points back to a previously visited node.

concepts.name: Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: The process of identifying whether a cycle exists within a linked list.

concepts.name: Tortoise and Hare
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that uses two pointers moving at different speeds to detect cycles.

concepts.name: Slow Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer that moves one step at a time in a linked list.

concepts.name: Fast Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A pointer that moves two steps at a time in a linked list.

concepts.name: Meeting Point
concepts.short_definition: The node where the slow and fast pointers meet if a cycle exists.

concepts.name: Cycle Start Node
concepts.short_definition: The first node in the linked list that is part of the cycle.

concepts.name: Finding Cycle Start
concepts.short_definition: The process of determining the entry point of a cycle in a linked list.

concepts.name: Hash Table
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that uses a hash function to map keys to values.

concepts.name: Visited Nodes
concepts.short_definition: Nodes that have been traversed during a linked list traversal.

concepts.name: Hash Set
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that stores unique elements and allows for efficient checking of membership.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The amount of memory an algorithm uses.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: The amount of time an algorithm takes to run.

concepts.name: Linear Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Visiting each node in a linked list sequentially from head to tail.

concepts.name: Infinite Loop
concepts.short_definition: A loop that continues indefinitely, often caused by a cycle in a linked list.

concepts.name: Single Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list where each node points only to the next node.

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list where each node points to both the next and previous nodes.

concepts.name: Circular Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list where the last node points back to the first node, forming a cycle.

concepts.name: Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: A set of rules or instructions to be followed in calculations or other problem-solving operations.

concepts.name: Data Structure
concepts.short_definition: A particular way of organizing and storing data in a computer.

concepts.name: Edge Case
concepts.short_definition: A specific scenario or input that might cause an algorithm to fail or behave unexpectedly.

concepts.name: Empty List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list with no nodes.

concepts.name: Single Node List
concepts.short_definition: A linked list containing only one node.

concepts.name: Cycle Length
concepts.short_definition: The number of nodes in a cycle.

concepts.name: Cycle Detection Methods
concepts.short_definition: Various techniques used to identify cycles in linked lists.

concepts.name: Two Pointers
concepts.short_definition: Using multiple pointers to traverse a data structure simultaneously.

concepts.name: Memory Usage
concepts.short_definition: The amount of computer memory required by a program or data structure.

concepts.name: Efficiency
concepts.short_definition: How well an algorithm or data structure performs in terms of time and space.

concepts.name: Node Manipulation
concepts.short_definition: Operations performed on nodes within a linked list, such as traversal or modification.

concepts.name: Pointer Movement
concepts.short_definition: The act of advancing pointers through a linked list.

concepts.name: Algorithm Analysis
concepts.short_definition: The process of evaluating the performance of an algorithm.

concepts.name: Problem Solving
concepts.short_definition: The process of finding solutions to complex issues.
